events.title: Thomas Newcomen builds a practical steam engine
John Kay invents the flying shuttle
James Hargreaves invents the spinning jenny
James Watt patents his improved steam engine
Adam Smith publishes The Wealth of Nations
Edmund Cartwright invents the power loom
Eli Whitney invents the cotton gin
Robert Fulton's steamboat begins regular service
Luddites begin smashing textile machinery
The first public steam railway opens in England
Michael Faraday demonstrates electromagnetic induction
Samuel Morse develops the telegraph
The first long-distance telegraph line opens
The Great Exhibition opens in London
Edwin Drake drills the first commercial oil well
The first successful transatlantic telegraph cable
The transcontinental railroad is completed
Alexander Graham Bell patents the telephone
Thomas Edison develops a practical light bulb
Karl Benz builds the first practical automobile
Marconi sends the first transatlantic radio signal
The Wright brothers achieve powered flight
Henry Ford introduces the Model T
Ford installs the first moving assembly line
